CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Hi Caroline, 


Your morning rate would be prime, higher cost to use the space. Your afternoon rate would be lower, because this is likely to be used less. So you have an AM member rate and AM Drop-in rate then you'll have a PM member rate and PM Drop-in rate. It really isn't an hourly rate but a "session rate" . Clients book the whole morning session and likewise book the whole afternoon session. It would be logistically difficult to have clients there for an hour or so. Yes, I think it will be difficult to ramp up and get users. You will also find varying demand by days of the week. 

The less complicated we can make it the better the clients will understand. Things like member sign-up deadlines before offering spots to drop-in. Billing for reserved time even if they don't come, you may let them have 1 occurrence of "sick day" a month if you are then able to fill in with a potential drop-in client.

I like you are actively i

I met with Taylor at Mid's Harrison campus to discuss the business start up process.

Originally, I gave Taylor a lean business plan to work on, but it seemed to be a little too big-picture for her to feel engaged. Instead, we decided to pivot to LivePlan. The new AI capabilities make the instructions more custom to each client based on their industry. This seemed to go over better, and Taylor was excited to start working on it.

We went through the narrative sections briefly, and I also showed her how to make adjustments to the forecast financial tables. We will work on these together as she moves forward.

A few other topics that we discussed are:

  • Basic market research - finding her audience in Gladwin
  • Commercial space for rent - potentially putting her in touch with MMDC for any leads
  • Professional referrals for both accountants and commercial insurance providers.

I will be doing some digging on these resources and emai

I drove up to visit Tom on Monday, 4/14. Tom has been working with Jeff, but needs assistance with SEO on his website. Additionally, he is interested in pursuing the Great Lakes Bay Small Business Hub grant opportunity.

Tom took the time to show me around the shop, including the lean-to's and shooting range on the property. I also met his wife, Jeanine, and Jeremy, his right-hand man.

We discussed the grant opportunity, and I clarified that building expenses would not be covered. This would put a damper on their plans to expand the shop and add a restroom, but the funding could be used for shipping equipment, if they are awarded.

Additionally, we discussed his website. Tom has quite a robust site, and his SEO value looks good already. He has to be careful about web hosts and card processing companies due to his industry, but has found providers that are reliable so far. I recommended that we start with an SEO audit from the Lead Center grad team as a baselin

Zoom with Gary after hearing he is pitching at TC New Tech tomorrow. He took a few minutes to describe how badly the tariffs are hitting his business. He lost a huge contract to supply a WalMart supplier already. He would have doubled his business. Then we rant through the pitch. I suggested he throw in the line about being soley US based, not reliant on trade to survive. He also showed me his training video for how to use the ear scanning app. I timed his pitch, he was dead on 5 minutes. He won't have a script tomorrow, so we'll see how it goes.


I may want to get some impact soon since we have this contact.

She does not like doing women because it takes longer and its much more complex. Why does she have to write an essay that is five pages long to express this simple plan? And she makes a great point. We discussed working backwards from there as she is starting today with building her brand, loyalty and following.

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Max Morefield and Nancy Benovich Gilby

Max Morefield and Nancy Benovich Gilby discussed app usability issues, including a bug that causes the app to appear pink when navigating from notifications or the profile. Nancy suggested adding a code for users to scan and enter their email without manual input. They also discussed enhancing the knowledge base with more visual content and adding videos on sexual health. Max plans to attend pride events for user sign-ups and is considering integrating AI for verifying test results, though it may be cost-prohibitive. Nancy advised forming a product advisory group for better engagement metrics. They also discussed HIPAA compliance and privacy frameworks.

Action Items

  • [ ] @Max Morefield - Implement the suggested change to the encounter details feature to allow users without the app to enter their email on their own device.
  • [ ] @Max Morefield - Investigate the HIPAA compliance requirements and work with Rapid Dev

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Client needed to better understand what to expect from meetings with potential investors. I told him top-line revenue and market penetration are at the top of the list. I refreshed him on how we get to revenue: find market size with link; if appropriate, find growth rate; decide pricing; apply s-curve with an appropriate definition of long-term (typically 10-15 years).


We then discussed COGS. Since the client is an engineer, he is completely on top of that. I reminded him how the Financial Summary is designed. he asked what he should send give potential investors on subsequent requests. I told him to just send the whole file.


I had to make a couple of changes/corrections, which I sent as version 4.3.

I started building the revenue model, subject to questions in our afternoon call. In that call, we decided that the GTM strategy would be selling systems for a couple of years followed by moving in hydrogen production.


We discussed basic assumptions, since the file they shared with me was loaded with inconsistencies. We then discussed a future transition to producing hydrogen in-house and selling small systems - or massively large systems - to customers using hydrogen rather than selling it.


I'll redo the file, including capitalization of equipment for internal use. Should be fun. 🙂

Becky emailed that she is in talks with a potential buyer and is working on a valuation. I've reached out to the growth team to get a team together to help with the transition and valuation. He didn't give me a figure but he even thought the 8-12 times gross revenue idea his friend gave him is a huge number (unrealistic).


So, I am waiting to receive their financials next week and am in touch with Jeff Punches.

Analysis: She has recently been awarded a $20K enhanced grant and is in the process of applying for an additional $5K grant. Additionally, she has submitted a loan application for $150K through Huntington Bank. Currently, she is focusing on rehabbing her building.

Recommendation: To ensure proper financial management, she should hire a bookkeeper to maintain accurate records, especially as she navigates multiple funding sources. Proper financial documentation will be crucial for managing the grants, loan, and any future financial transactions.

Next Steps:

  1. Hire a bookkeeper to ensure accurate financial record-keeping.
  2. Consider developing financial projections to better understand future cash flow and plan for growth.

Hi Kazie,

I've worked on your business plan. I've shared it with you as a shared Google Document. I've also included it here as an attachment.

I'm not able to go with you to your meeting at the bank. However, I can help you prepare for it. 


Some tips:

You want to give the bank a good impression that you have done your homework and researched the market. Using this business plan template shows that you have done so and that you have also worked with the SBDC. So it is worth taking your time to complete the business plan. You've already done a lot of the work.


The bank wants to know that you are a good risk.

I asked Mr. Long did he have plans to continue with his company and he indicated he did when and if he could either obtain a different vehicle or repair his current truck. Mr. Long indicated that he has the funds to have the truck repaired however, if he use the funds for the truck repairs it would deplete his savings and that makes him nervous. Media.Other: I found your website on a search engine

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
review LinkedIn page and provide feedback:

1. Feel free to share/repost content from other reputable industry sources. This will help increase your reach and visibility. Don't overdo it, but if you find a post with great messaging that aligns with your mission and values, give it a share. 

2. I think the length of your posts is good, but if you have shorter messaging or smaller nuggets to share, don't hesitate to do so. That way you can capture users with shorter attention spans. 

3. I know it's tough, but any way that you can humanize your content by including photos of you or partners/clients that you work with would be great. 

4. Other ideas include tying in community or place into your content if you can, i.e. if there is a business or organization holding an event that can be tied to safety, etc and encouraging engagement by asking questions/surveying, having people leave feedback on a topic, etc.

Ben is looking for accounting help and asking if I'll beta test the website. I sent him accounting resources,

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes: Summary/Action: The client and I talked by Zoom. His technology is a smoking/vaping cessation device that utilizes brain function instead of drug intervention. Kind of like Jvaping reaction in the brain without the nicotine with the sensation and taste of vaping but not the drug. He has an R21 application scheduled for review on April 23, 2025. His colleague, Dr. Brian Hitsman of Northwestern University, had submitted the application before the October 10, 2024 due date, with Dr. Amanda Mathew of Rush University. Since October, he has been in contact with FDA CDRH to determine whether an IDE is necessary for the R21. CAPNOS devices, at present, are sold to the general public in compliance with regulatory policies. His eventual goal is to achieve distribution opportunities with health insurance payers and quit programs as a recognized cessation device. The pre-submission for the IDE is also serving as a precursor for a 510k application to obtain messaging clearance for cessation clai

Met with Sharon and Allan Atwood. Previous to the meeting I checked over their website, did a Google search to check out their Google profile and Google ranking.

They are selling photos that Allan has taken of landscape scenery around Michigan. They sell primarily online with one shop in Silver City. Not sure of the story behind that. The website is quite navigable. However, they aren't sure how well the online purchasing process works. I recommended that Sharon try purchasing one or two prints and see how it works. CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
discussed the startup's progress, potential funding opportunities, and strategies for growth and exposure.

Anna provided an update on their startup, highlighting their revenue doubling, securing new clients, and negotiating a significant deal with a Canadian company. They also launched a redesigned and efficient INU app and are planning a press release. Despite positive growth, they are mindful of the challenges of bootstrapping and are now considering funding options to accelerate their development.

Nancy explained different funding avenues, including venture capital (which Anna is hesitant about due to the pressure and loss of control) and the Business Accelerator Fund (a grant up to $75,000). She clarified the permissible uses of the grant, emphasizing it can't be used for core feature development but can support areas like marketing, legal, design, and testing, potentially through individual consultants rather than just agencies.

This session covers meeting with BCU to discuss foods and beverage expertise as well as several text conversation with Paul regarding status of product launches in Meijer, Target and Kroger.

Summary:

Kevin, the founder of Vouch, a platform connecting content creators/influencers with brands, met with Nancy from the SBDC tech team to get advice and explore potential funding options. Kevin showcased his platform, explained its functionality, and discussed his go-to-market strategy. His immediate need is funding, as he has self-financed the development so far and is running out of resources to scale.

Key Points:

  • Vouch Platform: Connects content creators and brands.1
  • Creators can list products they "vouch" for in their own Vouch store, earning commission on sales. Brands can list products for free on the Vouch marketplace and set commission rates. It currently integrates with Shopify.2
  •   1. The Video Platform That's Easier To Use, Try Vouch

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Cybersecurity interest group - spoke about recent phishing relevant to this client (and others)


Meeting Summary

The meeting, moved to Microsoft Teams due to Zoom issues, focused primarily on an update regarding the SBDC's North Star Cybersecurity Certificate program, followed by an open forum discussion on cybersecurity experiences, challenges, and funding.

Marissa, the new Cybersecurity Program Manager for North Star (based in Delaware SBDC), presented the main update:

  • North Star 2025 Certification: The virtual training program for the 2025 certification cycle is now live. This is a no-cost, self-paced (~4-5 hour) training available to all SBDC staff and other SBA partners (WBCs, SCORE, etc.), culminating in an exam (80% pass rate required).
  • Deadline: To be certified for 2025, participants must complete the training and pass the exam by August 31, 2025.

Ben is very excited. He has built the website https://thef-hole.com/

John, the owner at String By Mail, told the staff there that Ben is still going to buy SBM. Ben told him not unless the price comes down. So, odd relationship there. Ben is keeping his job and will run TFH (The F Hole) at night, 1-7P Pacific time.


Ben has supplier relationships developed, and has his basement ready to operate from. He is relying on Google Shopping to market and has a daily budget.


He's going to share a version of the website so I can beta test it.

Meeting Summary

This meeting served as an update session between the founder (referred to as Ryan) of a platform likely called "Workcases" and his advisors, Nancy and Joe Gilby. It covered significant technical strategy shifts, ecosystem collaborations, and future needs.

Key discussion points included:

  • Technical Pivot: Ryan detailed a major shift (~3-month delay) from UI testing to rebuilding the backend data infrastructure using PostgreSQL (instead of Azure SQL) and bringing AI processing (vectorization, RAG) in-house with open-source models and dedicated hardware (NVIDIA). This was driven by AI readiness needs and substantial projected cost savings compared to cloud provider services. Other tools like Wild Duck (email), Directus (BaaS), and OCR/parsing tools were mentioned as part of the new stack. The target for a beta product launch is now 3-4 months out.
  • New Features: The infrastructure

Areen has been doing permanant makeup for 5+ years in a home studio she and her husband built during the pandemic. She feels they could earn more money and start teaching if she had more space.


They are starting on the process to expand the business and open a studio where other artists can work and Areen can teach.


Her business partner is he husband, Sameer.


In this meeting we went over liveplan and focus four as tools to help make the expansion possible.

Meeting Summary:

The meeting covered updates on several clients and procedural discussions, particularly concerning Business Accelerator Fund (BAF) applications.

  1. Fleetwood: An SOW is being processed. Toni will monitor the electronic signing process. Fleetwood is confirmed as a Michigan company eligible for BAF submission, which participants hope will help them succeed in a new market.

Quick recap 

Nancy advised Ryan on how to refine his customer discovery process for his travel app idea, emphasizing the importance of targeting a specific audience with urgent needs and conducting in-person interviews without revealing the product idea. She also stressed the value of recruiting 10 people with critical needs before developing a prototype and offered to share resources to help with data collection. Additionally, Nancy mentioned the possibility of applying for a $5,000 grant to build a prototype once the customer discovery process is complete.


Next steps

  • Ryan to refine target audience for his travel app, focusing on a specific subset of families with urgent needs.
  • Ryan to conduct in-person interviews with potential users without disclosing his product idea.
  • Ryan to recruit 10 people who self-rank their travel-related problems as 1 or 2 on the urgency scale.

Summary

Thank you very much Julie. I will take a look at this file and fill it out as best as I can.


I will say that, after briefly looking over the other documents, our expense trajectory is not quite right. One of the beauties of our new business is that our expenses are pretty set and won't increase by much as our student count and our revenue grows. Our rent, our utilities, our marketing expenses, our supplies, etc. will all pretty much stay the same as we get more and more students. Our rent will increase by like 2 or 3% each year, our royalties expense will increase and our instructor payroll will increase, but that's about it!


Based on my numbers, we will "break even" at about 35 students and we're already at 18 after 3 months. So we're already halfway there (and this includes paying $1,200 - $1,500 per month for a loan payment) and everything above and beyond that will be pure profit. At 50(ish) students, my w

Hi Allison,


I am sorry we were not able to talk on Monday, you have a lot on your plate at the moment. I am so sorry you are going through all this. I wish I could be more helpful but you definitely need to find a lawyer to help you with this. I am not sure what your operating agreement states, but I would think he can not hold you hostage in ownership of the Inn. Have you checked out the U of M Law School? They may be able to refer you to an attorney that specializes in this.


I understand why Shonna thinks you should have a business valuation but honestly, the business is worth the property value. Since you haven’t been profitable, your first decision to list the building was correct, it was just very unfortunate that sabotaged the process.


Try the law school and also look at the Ann Arbor-Ypsi Chamber website to find the right person.


All the best,

AN ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM: He needs help with getting fresh cash and general business management

 

RECOMMENDATIONS AND ACTIONS TO BE TAKEN: I Zoomed with Leanne, Sheery, TJ, and Tamara with Metro about this case. We shared information about his recent activities and financial information. 640 credit score...ok with Metro, collateral...ok with Metro. I also brought up taking second mortgages on real estate. Leanne asked about getting training attendance certificates...receipts, and I will check this out. Metro is willing to do a Micro loan for up to $50000 for 6 years at 8%. Sheery will need the balance sheet and tax returns that Leanne will provide. Timing is about a month to make sure that due diligence is being followed.

Analysis: William is actively attending another class and, after each session, schedules an appointment with me to discuss his next steps. His dedication and motivation are apparent as he is eager to elevate his business. Having completed his six points, he is now ready to write his executive summary. Currently, he is conducting marketing research to understand why he should receive a grant for his business.

Recommendation: To gather valuable insights, William should conduct a survey to determine whether there is demand for his products. This will help validate his business idea and provide data to support his grant application. Understanding his target audience's needs and preferences is crucial for both his marketing strategy and grant proposal.

Next Steps:

  1. Complete the survey to gather feedback and insights from potential customers.
  2. Finalize the brand organizer to clarify his brand identity and
I talked to the client and he confirmed that he is working with Nancy Gilby. Further updated the profile based on the information provided by the client..

Alejendro called out of the blue to ask about financing for a project in Mexico. He needs $210,000 to buy a geo membrane. The total project cost is $550k. He has a PO already. He has some money. He needs the mining company to put down 28%. I suggested he focus on Mexican banks for now since that's where the project is located. He doesn't have many years in busienss, and he has friends who could lend him the money. He will get it done, but financing through a bank seems unlikely. But we could try, but his timing may not allow that much time. His business is based in Birmingham MI, the prodcut is coming from Quatar, and is being installed in northern Mexico. He could earn a margin of 35%.


Quick call, but will be in touch.

Assigned Consultant

Reviewed the list of market research questions from Robert's recent emails. He requested information regarding quick-service restaurants and business valuation services. I did some research and sent him two market research reports compiling answers to his questions concerning each business.

Met with client to determine if we are the appropriate group in the SBDC to assist. While the company has been in existence since 2011, they have a new project that needs our help. The existing business is a consultancy; the project is product.


As the company name implies, they are in the chemical controls space. They are deeply imbedded in manufacturing in SE Michigan. As the client pointed out, nearly everything on the plant floor has been automated, except chemical controls. It seems that chemical companies ship concentrate to their customers for various applications, whether as lubricants, solvents, or undercoating.


Controlling mixes and concentrations is currently a manual process. But the manufacturing environment is not static. Currently, workers are expected to test regularly during a shift to ensure proper adjustments. But this implies time lags between inspections, and often, the adjustments are wrong.


The client has auto

Analysis: Fatima is diligently balancing her job while coming up with ideas for her business. She remains committed to her goal of selling apparel and is currently working on her business plan. She reached out for a check-in to stay accountable as she progresses with her entrepreneurial goals.

Recommendation: Fatima should continue focusing on creating her apparel and begin researching potential manufacturers who can supply the products. Identifying reliable manufacturers is crucial for scaling her business. Additionally, working on her social media presence will help build brand awareness and attract her target audience.

Next Steps:

  1. Research manufacturers to find reliable suppliers for her apparel line.
  2. Focus on developing her social media strategy to start promoting her brand and engaging with potential customers.
